raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 94 would have allowed individuals sentenced to Life Without Parole before June 5, 1990, to petition for resentencing after serving a minimum of 25 years.",
    "The proposed amendments to SB 94 aimed to address concerns from law enforcement agencies and included a narrowed scope of the bill.",
    "SB 94 would have established a more conservative and thorough process for identifying rehabilitated individuals in the incarcerated population.",
    "Individuals who were victims of human trafficking, under the age of 26, or veterans may have received excessively harsh sentences inconsistent with current judicial standards.",
    "The review process proposed by SB 94 offered significantly more oversight compared to the current powers held by the Governor to commute and pardon sentences.",
    "SB 94 would not release any individuals from prison and would not pose a risk to public safety."
  ]
}
```
